author: frangio_
content:
yeah i merged my PR since it's more direct, EAS/geas needs a pragma to avoid emitting push0 afaik

<@508125616940515329> i think your point about the nonce is worth considering, but i don't think there's a requirement on EIP-161, it's just a background assumption because otherwise all the "set nonce to 1" part of the spec is just not necessary. but if that EIP is activated that part of the spec is needed, so we can either make it conditional, or leave it as is, since it shouldn't be harmful?

author: jochembrouwer
content:
You are right, EIP-161 is indeed not required (it can be reasoned back as the background assumption for the irregular state transition to raise nonce to 1 if nonce of account is 0). But EIP-161 is not a requirement for this EIP, as it is self contained. (I reasoned about this from the wrong direction, so yes EIP-161 is indeed not required)

FYI, I opened my [PR](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11856) with the bytecode in same style as the other system contracts https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7002, https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-2935, https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7251, https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-4788. 
EIP-7997 now introduces a slightly different style to represent the bytecode.
